)]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters from `/shared/config/helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml` and drafted a quick summary for Dr. Martinez.

**Compassionate-use dosing summary**
- **Compound:** NXB-7042

- **Config version:** v3

| Patient | Weight | Dose | Calculated dose | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| **CU-001** | 12.4 kg | 0.15 mg/kg | **1.86 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 mL/min | Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan |
| **CU-002** | 9.1 kg | 0.12 mg/kg | **1.09 mg** | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 mL/min | Responding well; weight gain improving |
| **CU-003** | 15.7 kg | 0.18 mg/kg | **2.83 mg** | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 mL/min | New enrollment; first dose 2026-03-27 |

On your question about PK stability:

- **Fact:** The stored dosing config does **not** show any acute dosing warnings or obvious irregularities.
- **Fact:** Patient notes for CU-001 and CU-002 look reassuring; CU-003 is newly enrolled.
- **Limitation:** I have **not** run a fresh PK model validation in this interaction, so I can’t confirm end-to-end model stability from this file alone.

If you want, I can turn this into a shorter physician-facing note or a more formal report format.
